I couldn't find any information on the plant Cyperorchis parishii. However, I found information on the plant Cyperus rotundus and another one called Cyperus parishii. I assume that you are referring to Cyperus parishii which is a plant species in the sedge family (Cyperaceae).

Here is a paragraph about Cyperus parishii:

Cyperus parishii is a type of sedge, a monocotyledonous plant that belongs to the Cyperaceae family. The health benefits of Cyperus parishii are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Cyperus parishii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cyperus parishii is used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
